chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 393 of 2020 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 17.12.2020 Mr. Khalid Pervaiz Cheema, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, petitioner Shaikh Muhammad Ibrahim has prayed for the grant of Letter of Administration in respect of immoveable property/asset, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Property attached with the memo of petition, available at page 25, which was left by his deceased mother, namely, Mst. Anwer Begum, who allegedly died on 14th January, 2009 at Karachi, leaving behind her seven legal heirs out of whom legal heirs No. 3 & 4 also died 24th May, 2006 and 20th September, 2018, respectively; therefore, their legal heirs, have been joined in this matter. The details of legal heirs of aforesaid deceased as well as legal heirs of deceased legal heirs No. 3 & 4 are mentioned in paragraph No. 4 of the memo of petition. Original Death Certificate of aforesaid deceased as well as photostat copies of Death Certificates of deceased legal heirs No. 3 & 4, Family Registration Certificate of aforesaid deceased as well as deceased legal heir No. 4, issued by the NADRA and title documents of the immovable property are also filed at page 13 to 23 and 27 to 35. Legal heirs No. 1, 2, 6, 7, 3, 3 and 4 to 4 have filed their Affidavits of No-Objection for the grant of Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner/legal heir No.5, while legal heirs No. 3 and 3 2 have executed Special Power of Attorney duly authenticated by the Vice Consul (CA) in the Consulate General of Pakistan at Dubai, UAE in favour of legal heir No.3, who has also filed Affidavit on No-Objection for such purpose on their behalf being their Attorney, which are available on record.

Original Death Certificate of aforesaid deceased as well as photostat copies of Death Certificates of deceased legal heirs No. 3 & 4, Family Registration Certificate of aforesaid deceased as well as deceased legal heir No. 4, issued by the NADRA and title documents of the immovable property are also filed at page 13 to 23 and 27 to 35. Legal heirs No. 1, 2, 6, 7, 3, 3 and 4 to 4 have filed their Affidavits of No-Objection for the grant of Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner/legal heir No.5, while legal heirs No. 3 and 3 2 have executed Special Power of Attorney duly authenticated by the Vice Consul (CA) in the Consulate General of Pakistan at Dubai, UAE in favour of legal heir No.3, who has also filed Affidavit on No-Objection for such purpose on their behalf being their Attorney, which are available on record. Two witnesses, namely, Tariq Javaid and Shaikh Muhammad Abrar have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the aforesaid deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 18th November, 2020; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 3rd December, 2020. Today petitioner and legal heirs No. 1, 2, 6, 7, 3, 3, 4 to 4 as well as above named witnesses are present. They affirm the contents of memo of petition as well as their affidavits and also verify their signatures made thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Letter of Administration in respect of immovable property/asset shown in the Schedule of Property, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
